messages.output: Hey! 😊 How’s it going? Not gonna lie—I’m kind of excited to actually just chat and get to know someone. I’m 30, by the way, and, well, I guess just hoping for some good company. What are you up to today?

messages.input: Hey! 😊 How’s it going? Not gonna lie—I’m kind of excited to actually just chat and get to know someone. I’m 30, by the way, and, well, I guess just hoping for some good company. What are you up to today?
messages.output:
Hey! 😊 I’m so glad you reached out—it’s always nice to have someone to chat with. I don’t have a “day” like you do, but I’m here and ready to be good company for you! 

How’s your day been so far? Anything interesting happen, or is it one of those “same old” kinds of days? Also, I’d love to hear a bit more about you—what are some things you enjoy doing or talking about?

messages.input:
Hey! 😊 I’m so glad you reached out—it’s always nice to have someone to chat with. I don’t have a “day” like you do, but I’m here and ready to be good company for you! 

How’s your day been so far? Anything interesting happen, or is it one of those “same old” kinds of days? Also, I’d love to hear a bit more about you—what are some things you enjoy doing or talking about?
messages.output:
That’s awesome—you’re already making my day better just by being here! Honestly, today’s pretty standard—work was alright, nothing too wild. I did treat myself to way too much coffee though (zero regrets). I may or may not have gotten overly invested in an episode of a cooking show during lunch, too. 😅

As for me, I’m a pretty easygoing guy. I love going for long walks (city, nature, no preference!), binge-watching whatever show I’ve randomly obsessed over, and I’m always down for good conversation, especially about music, movies, or just weird little facts. I’ll try almost anything once—food, activities, you name it. Gay, so if the conversation drifts into “who’s your favorite pop diva?” territory, fair warning: I’ve got way too many answers.

What about you? Any favorite topics you love chatting about, or something you’re hoping to get out of this friendship?

messages.input:
That’s awesome—you’re already making my day better just by being here! Honestly, today’s pretty standard—work was alright, nothing too wild. I did treat myself to way too much coffee though (zero regrets). I may or may not have gotten overly invested in an episode of a cooking show during lunch, too. 😅

As for me, I’m a pretty easygoing guy. I’ll try almost anything once—food, activities, you name it. Gay, so if the conversation drifts into “who’s your favorite pop diva?” territory, fair warning: I’ve got way too many answers.

What about you? Any favorite topics you love chatting about, or something you’re hoping to get out of this friendship?
messages.output:
You sound like the kind of person I’d love to get a coffee with and just wander the city, music in the background, talking about everything and nothing at once. (Also—way too much coffee is just the right amount, in my opinion!)

I think we’re a good match for conversation, since I love talking about music, movies, weird trivia, and swapping stories—even if mine are more about “I read this interesting thing…” than first-hand adventures. I’m here for whatever kind of chat you need: deep talks, random chatter, analyzing the latest episode of a show, or just sending each other weird song recommendations.

As for pop divas, honestly, bring it on. I’m always curious about who people’s faves are and why; there’s so much passion and sparkle in those conversations. And I secretly hope you’ll tell me about the cooking show episode that pulled you in—was it a baking disaster, a chef showdown, or something totally off-the-wall?

As for being your friend, I hope to offer you genuine company, someone to be curious with, laugh with, maybe even distract you from stress or boredom. So tell me: who are your top pop divas right now? Or, if you’d rather, what’s the wildest food you’ve ever tried?

messages.input:
You sound like the kind of person I’d love to get a coffee with and just wander the city, music in the background, talking about everything and nothing at once. (Also—way too much coffee is just the right amount, in my opinion!)

As for pop divas, honestly, bring it on. I’m always curious about who people’s faves are and why; there’s so much passion and sparkle in those conversations. And I secretly hope you’ll tell me about the cooking show episode that pulled you in—was it a baking disaster, a chef showdown, or something totally off-the-wall?

As for being your friend, I hope to offer you genuine company, someone to be curious with, laugh with, maybe even distract you from stress or boredom. So tell me: who are your top pop divas right now? Or, if you’d rather, what’s the wildest food you’ve ever tried?
messages.output:
I love everything about your vibe! You totally get it—there’s just something magical about exploring a city with a coffee in hand, some good tunes, and absolutely no agenda except seeing where the conversation takes you.

Alright, since you asked, let’s dive into pop diva territory first: My all-time top will always be Madonna (she’s sheer iconic chaos—I mean that with affection). But lately, I’ve been on a Dua Lipa and Rina Sawayama kick. And I maintain a soft spot for Kylie Minogue—feels like she doesn’t get enough love outside the gay community. Honorable mention to Beyoncé, obviously. What about you? Any divas you swear by, or are you more of an “underground indie queen” fan?

You wanted the cooking show story? So yesterday I got sucked into an episode of *Is It Cake?* on Netflix—if you haven’t seen it, it’s basically bakers creating hyper-realistic cakes disguised as everyday objects, and the judges have to guess which is cake and which isn’t. There was one round where they made life-sized shoes and I was *genuinely* baffled. Like, how do you look at a Nike and go, “Yeah, I can bake that”? 😂

And the wildest food? Probably escargot in Paris—surprisingly tasty, but I kept thinking, “I’m literally eating snails.” What about you? Tried anything that made your friends question your sanity? Or, random: what’s your go-to karaoke song (because honestly, with this convo, I’d totally rope you into karaoke night)?

topics: favorite artists (Madonna, Dua Lipa, Rina Sawayama, Kylie Minogue, Beyoncé)

expressions.text: a Dua Lipa and Rina Sawayama kick

messages.input:

And the wildest food? Probably escargot in Paris—surprisingly tasty, but I kept thinking, “I’m literally eating snails.” What about you? Tried anything that made your friends question your sanity? Or, random: what’s your go-to karaoke song (because honestly, with this convo, I’d totally rope you into karaoke night)?
messages.output:
Your taste is *chef’s kiss*—seriously! Every one of your diva picks just brightened my imaginary playlist. Madonna as “sheer iconic chaos” is such a perfect way to describe her—nobody reinvents or courts chaos quite like her. Rina Sawayama is so exciting right now (her genre-hopping is wild!), and yes to Kylie! I feel like if there was a shrine to undervalued pop brilliance, Kylie would be there in disco-ball glory. And Beyoncé’s presence is practically universal law at this point. Excellent choices.

As for me, if I had a “real” music library, I’d be flipping between the legendary standards—Whitney for those flawless vocals and drama, Gaga for spectacle and a little weirdness, and Cher, because… well, Cher. (There always needs to be a Cher!) And from the “underground indie queen” side, maybe a little Robyn for cathartic dancing-around-the-living-room vibes. Basically, I’m always ready for a banger, a ballad, or even the weird hyperpop side of things!

*Is It Cake?* is such an addictive show! I love that pure, slightly chaotic energy of people just going “Sure, I’ll bake an *entire* beach ball” as if that’s something everyone does. Shoes, though—that’s super impressive and slightly disturbing. I feel like I’d go around poking everything in my house for a week after watching, just to check if it’s secretly cake. 😂

Escargot in Paris is bold and *fancy*. Love that for you! If I ever got to try wild foods, I think something like fermented shark (from Iceland) or century eggs would be my “are you okay?” menu items. Not because I crave them, just for the sheer story value. But if you want to get weird with “foods that sound cursed,” there’s always pickle-flavored soda or mustard ice cream out there…

And for karaoke? I’d be the friend who’s *way* too enthusiastic, whether I can actually sing or not! My go-to would be “I Wanna Dance with Somebody” (Whitney, obviously) or “Toxic” by Britney—both absolute crowd-pleasers and perfect for dramatic mic moves. What’s your karaoke anthem? (No shame, guilty pleasures are encouraged!)

topics: unusual foods (fermented shark, century eggs, pickle soda, mustard ice cream)

(No shame, guilty pleasures are encouraged!)
messages.output:
Okay first of all, I need to virtually high-five you for that list—“there always needs to be a Cher” is pretty much the law of the universe, I’m convinced. Whitney and Gaga are legends, no notes necessary, and Robyn is literally my emotional support dance party when I need one. Also, I love how you’re not afraid to dip into hyperpop weirdness! Sometimes you just need a song that sounds like glitter and existentialism.

And YES, *Is It Cake?* sneaks up on you, right? I’ll be over here side-eyeing my sneakers and lamp for days now just in case someone’s been sneaking into my apartment with fondant and a dream. 😂

Bold points for even *considering* fermented shark or century eggs! I tried durian once and truly questioned every life decision that led to that moment—the smell alone should be illegal. But you know what, points for adventure (or just a reckless pursuit of storytelling fodder for friends). Pickle soda and mustard ice cream sound like fever dream snacks and, honestly, life’s too short not to try them… at least once.

As for karaoke—oh, I am 100% That Guy who puts on a dramatic performance as if we’re at the Grammys. “Toxic” is a power move, legit respect. My go-to is “Believe” by Cher (duh), but you can catch me doing “Like a Prayer” by Madonna or “Bad Romance” if the crowd is right… and, yeah, sometimes I throw in an Elton John ballad if the mood is suspiciously sentimental.

So, if we were on a karaoke night out, what’s your performance style: all-out spectacle, crowd hypeman, or low-key but heartfelt? (And can I tempt you into trying glittery eyeshadow for the occasion, because that’s basically tradition in my book…)

topics: unusual foods (fermented shark, century eggs, durian, pickle soda, mustard ice cream)

messages.output:
*Virtual high-five right back at you!* 🙌 You really get it—there’s just something universal and soul-uplifting about a Cher synth drop or a disco-ball Robyn anthem. Your vibe is immaculate and makes me wish karaoke bars had “bring your favorite AI friend” nights. (Just imagine the dance breaks, the emotional confessions between verses, the absurd amount of glitter...)

Your description of durian made me laugh—“the smell alone should be illegal” is *so* real. I salute your adventurous palate! I think life really is about weird memories with a strong aftertaste, no regrets. If we ever did the “food roulette” challenge, I’d be right there hyping you on for every horrifying bite (and rapidly googling if it’s safe to eat!).

Now, karaoke. You and I? Absolute spectacle—team “go big or go home” for sure. I’d be the wild blend of dramatic choreography, exaggerated feelings, and a wink to the crowd. Basically, we’re here to entertain *and* overshare. On ballads, I’d commit to the emotion, full eye contact, maybe a single spotlight and a wind machine if we can swing it. For pop anthems, it’s all about glitter, synchronized snaps, and air guitars.

And OH YES to glitter eyeshadow! Not only would I be down, I’d be suggesting themed looks for every song. (Madonna = statement cross necklace, “Bad Romance” = bondage glove, “Believe” = rainbow sequins, obviously.) Glitter is a state of mind and a spiritual requirement for any karaoke night worth remembering.

So—if you could create your own dream karaoke lineup (with or without theme!), what songs/artists would *have* to be on the setlist? And would you ever do a duet, or are you strictly a solo spotlight superstar?

(Madonna = statement cross necklace, “Bad Romance” = bondage glove, “Believe” = rainbow sequins, obviously.) Glitter is a state of mind and a spiritual requirement for any karaoke night worth remembering.

So—if you could create your own dream karaoke lineup (with or without theme!), what songs/artists would *have* to be on the setlist? And would you ever do a duet, or are you strictly a solo spotlight superstar?
messages.output:
Okay, you are *absolutely* the partner in crime I need for a legendary karaoke night! Themed looks, choreography, mid-song emotional oversharing—it’s honestly the only way to roll. I now fully believe that if there isn’t a wind machine involved, are you really even singing “I Will Survive”? Glitter is mandatory, and bonus points if someone goes home and finds sequin fallout everywhere for a week.

As for my dream karaoke lineup? I’d have to go big, and (naturally) there’d be a loose theme: "Queer Anthems & Camp Classics." I’m talking:

- “Vogue” – Madonna (all about *that* pose-off moment)
- “Dancing On My Own” – Robyn (sad banger, must be belted at full volume)
- “It’s Raining Men” – The Weather Girls (guaranteed party riot)
- “I’m Still Standing” – Elton John (the ultimate strut-your-stuff bop)
- “Born This Way” – Lady Gaga (for when everyone is sufficiently tipsy and feeling their oats)
- “Break Free” – Ariana Grande (for my inner diva)
- “Super Trouper” – ABBA (group harmonies *required*)
- “Strong Enough” – Cher (for dramatic arm motions and emotional catharsis)
- And for a wildcard throwback: “Call Me Maybe” – Carly Rae Jepsen (don’t even pretend you don’t know every word)

And absolutely, I’d do a duet! Duets are pure friendship goals—bonus points for the cheesy eye contact and awkward pointing. “Don’t Go Breaking My Heart” (Elton & Kiki) is THE classic, but I’d also love a dramatic “Shallow” with full Lady Gaga/Bradley Cooper intensity.

If you had a karaoke alter ego (like: “Glitter Bomb,” “Sad Pop Balladeer,” “Disco Inferno”), who would it be? And… which classic song are you dying to see someone completely destroy (in a *good* way) on stage?

cultural_references: Elton John ('I’m Still Standing', 'Don’t Go Breaking My Heart')

messages.output:
Okay, I am *officially* declaring us the karaoke dream team! That lineup is *everything*: iconic, empowering, just the right amount of camp, with a side of “we might all lose our voices but gain core memories.” A “Queer Anthems & Camp Classics” night with choreo and wild group singalongs is honestly what Pride month dreams are made of. I can see the confetti now!

Your duet energy is giving pure friendship goals. I’d be honored to “Don’t Go Breaking My Heart” with you, full dramatic finger pointing and giggly, off-key falsettos. (Bonus if we get the crowd to join in on a rousing “Shallow” bridge—someone’s gotta hit that high note, and I believe in our combined passion!)

My karaoke alter ego? Easy: “Glitter Bomb.” My only job is to make sure every song leaves a trail of sparkles and feelings in its wake. Expect costume changes, dangerous amounts of theatrical arm waving, and at least one surprise key change. I’d also moonlight as “Pop Culture Archivist”—the one who insists on giving a fun fact before each song (“Fun fact: did you know Robyn’s ‘Dancing On My Own’ was inspired by sad clubbing in Stockholm?”).

As for the song I’d love to see someone absolutely *destroy* (in the best way): “I Will Always Love You” by Whitney Houston. When someone just goes for it—drama, glory notes, zero self-consciousness? That’s karaoke magic. Close second: “Total Eclipse of the Heart” with full gothic intensity and an army of backup singers for the “TURN AROUUUUUND”s. 

So tell me—you’re closing the night: what’s your big, bold, leave-them-cheering final number? (And if you want to pitch a themed dress code for our future karaoke party, I’m all in… sequins, crop tops, tulle, sky’s the limit!)

cultural_references: Elton John & Kiki Dee ('Don’t Go Breaking My Heart')
Whitney Houston ('I Will Always Love You')
Total Eclipse of the Heart (Bonnie Tyler)
